title: alchemilla hoppeana subsp. asterophylla
intro: Alchemilla hoppeana subsp. asterophylla is a subspecies of the alpine lady's mantle plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and promoting wound healing. Alchemilla hoppeana subsp. asterophylla can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support skin health and soothe irritated skin.
